transcript.clean_text: stage two of the Tour de France saw the happy ending to one of cycling's most difficult stories in the last few years a win for Fabio Jacobson on Tour de France debut just less than two years after he almost died in a crash at the tour of Poland but for not we'll pull on the yellow jersey for the first time in his career and lead the Tour de France going into stage three ahead of evil Lombard by a single second stage three began in Violet and will take the Riders to sunnyboy another brilliant date bumper crowds out there and all the jerseys lined up and ready to go with minus caught in the polka dots of the king of the mountains 182 kilometers with three more classified climbs on the journey South finishing in sonobot menu for a tour of Denmark stage finished last year won by The Local Hero Mass pitherson it was another Dane the Great Dane of day two Magnus Court who was again up the road and nobody followed him a lone attack in the first few pedal Strokes telegraphed by his Sports director saying he was going to go for the points again nobody though choose to go on the attack with him not even one of the teams invited to the race by the organizers it was all very relaxed in the Peloton as Court went on to pull in all of the Applause and the adulation all for himself six minutes 18 seconds when he went over the first categorized climb of the day already guaranteeing his position as the king of the mountains for day four the intermediate Sprint Was Won by Wild fanart Fabio Jacobson was up there with him taking second fanart in control of the green jersey classification despite having yellow on his own back as the stage went on it would be another big moment for miners Court three out of three six out of six Every Mountain climb classified in this tour so far won by the same Rider six points and taking the jersey at least into Thursday most probably he'd be caught with 52 kilometers to go after a one-man Danish show on home roads received by crowds we hadn't seen for a long long time at this race things continue to get nervous no Breakaway for the last 50ks into the final 20ks and that fight for position at the front was a mixture of GC Riders and sprinters all trying to get in position for the running into sonnerboard 11 kilometers to go still the Danish Flags waved but the wind not blowing too strongly just 12 13 kilometers per hour that didn't however stop a nasty crash quite a few Riders caught up in it and a few GC Riders would lose time down by the harbor with six K's to go quick step out for vinyl looking to be the first ever team to win three stages in the opening three stages a hat-trick possible maybe inside the last three kilometers to go those who've made it into the front were able to breathe a sigh of relief with everybody getting the same time in this group had there been any incident thankfully there hadn't but those caught up in the original one would lose a little bit of time one kilometer to go will be various teams fighting for the front the French champion doing that fantastically well a final corner with 750 meters to the line that was won by a quick step Alpha vinyl yombo visma were there as well however made this again in position the Sprint launched Laport leading out for that segan getting close Yoon boxed in he was angry again behind and as Phillipson went to the line one man was faster than them all and that was de la corunavega coming through the center and winning a stage of the Tour de France for the first time in three years oh yeah very very close on the line but the Dutchman getting the better of his former teammate fanart and bike exchange having their first win at this Tour de France on day three Phillipson Sagan jakobson the top five with La Porte dining all in the top ten there's a little bit of finger-pointing going on at some point Watson art kept the yellow Jersey and he will take it to France for day four on Tuesday and this is his lead seven seconds now over evil on part with the bonus seconds put in there bogacha Remains The Best of the general classification contenders Peterson and thunderpool making up the top five so to stage four and after a transfer and rest day on Monday on Tuesday it's 171.5 kilometers in northern France starting in Dunkirk and heading up to Cali plenty of classified climbs available opportunity may be to equal at the top there for one Rider Magnus caught in the king of the mountains classification but nervous roads twisting and turning again they'll have to watch for the wind for now what's on our leads heading into stage four
